label: Energy-based objectives

node.label: Energy-based objectives
node.definition: A scalar energy ranks compatible configurations lower than incompatible ones.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Energy-based objectives is modeled as depending on Generative modeling, Probability theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models.
examples: atlas downstream link to Energy-based, Hopfield & Boltzmann models
limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Generative modeling, Probability theory.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Energy-based objectives has a `uses` dependency on Generative modeling because Generative modeling is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Energy-based objectives has a `uses` dependency on Probability theory because Probability theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Energy-based objectives has a `uses` dependency on Optimization theory because Optimization theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Generative modeling, Probability theory.
